api_changes.reason: Replace TOM PWMHL API with GTM high-level PWM API per user request
Replace TOM PWMHL API with GTM high-level PWM API per user request
User requested use of IfxGtm_Pwm_setOnTime for duty updates
Equivalent high-level GTM PWM update API
Header replace required by requested driver swap

peripheral_requirement.purpose: Generate 3-phase center-aligned PWM for inverter (preserve existing TOM/timer mapping and pin mappings). Use high-level GTM PWM API per user request.

pins_and_mapping.note: Keep original TOM/timer assignments and pin maps from reference. Do not remap IfxPort headers.

timing_requirements.duty_wrapping_behavior: increment each channel by +10% of period on each update; if >100% then set to 0%

code_structure.notes: Preserve function names and overall file layout from the reference project. Update implementation to call IfxGtm_Pwm_* APIs while keeping original exported function names.

peripheral_usage.Port: pin configuration (preserve existing port header usage)

user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Implement a 3-phase, center-aligned PWM motor control for the TC387 reference inverter using the unified GTM high-level PWM driver, migrating from the TOM PWMHL driver while preserving original TOM/timer mapping and pin mappings.
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Initialize the three PWM channels (U,V,W) with duties U=25%, V=50%, W=75%; run center-aligned PWM at PWM_FREQ_HZ = 20000 Hz with dead time = 0.5e-6 s and min pulse = 1.0e-6 s; on each periodic update (WAIT_TIME = 500 ms) increase each channel duty by +10% of period and wrap to 0% when exceeding 100%; preserve existing TOM/timer mapping and pin mappings (no pin remap).
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use the unified IfxGtm_Pwm high-level API (IfxGtm_Pwm_initConfig + IfxGtm_Pwm_init) to configure the GTM PWM cluster in center-aligned mode and preserve TOM/timer mapping and pin mapping from the reference. Use IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelDutyImmediate to apply runtime duty changes (mapped from the reference setOnTime semantics). Guard GTM module enablement with IfxGtm_isEnabled / IfxGtm_enable and use IfxPort_* for any pin function setup. Keep the reference function names (initGtmTom3phInv, updateGtmTom3phInvDuty) to preserve the external API.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Header selection was mandated and the IfxGtm_Pwm unified high-level driver provides the required GTM PWM configuration and duty update APIs available in the template. It is the correct high-level replacement for the reference IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl and is locked by the migration specification. It also supports center-aligned PWM semantics and runtime duty updates (updateChannelDutyImmediate) which map to the reference on-time updates.
alternatives_considered: IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl (legacy - replaced by unified GTM PWM)

init_function.name: initGtmTom3phInv
init_function.purpose: Reference-preserved initialization that calls IfxGtm_Pwm_initConfig + IfxGtm_Pwm_init, configures center-aligned mode, sets PWM frequency (20 kHz), dead time (0.5e-6 s) and initial duties (25%,50%,75%), and configures pins using existing pin mappings.

runtime_function.name: updateGtmTom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Called periodically (WAIT_TIME = 500 ms from Cpu0_Main.c). For each channel (U,V,W) compute new duty = duty + 10%; if new duty > 100% set duty = 0%; apply new duty immediately using IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelDutyImmediate (maps to requested setOnTime semantics).

PWM.rationale: Driver locked by refiner node (user explicit or engine-scored)

public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize and configure a 3-channel, center-aligned GTM PWM cluster for a 3-phase inverter and store persistent module state for runtime updates. Implementation steps (in order): 1) Declare all local configuration structures (IfxGtm_Pwm_Config, IfxGtm_Pwm_ChannelConfig[channelCount], any output/deadtime arrays) as local variables. 2) Call IfxGtm_Pwm_initConfig(&config, &MODULE_GTM) to populate defaults. 3) Set config fields: set channelCount = 3 (one logical channel per phase), set frequency = PWM_FREQ_HZ (20 000 Hz), set center-aligned/counter mode via the config counter mode field, set deadtime = PWM_DEAD_TIME (0.5e-6 s), set minPulse = PWM_MIN_PULSE_TIME (1.0e-6 s), set output mode and pad driver (push-pull / automotive pad driver) and set each channel's polarity/active state as required. 4) Initialize per-channel defaults by calling IfxGtm_Pwm_initChannelConfig for each channelConfig[i] and assign any channel-specific parameters (phases/duty placeholders). 5) Prepare initial dutyPercent array in module state with U=25.0, V=50.0, W=75.0 (store these percent values directly in the persistent dutyCycles array in the module-state). 6) Enable guard: check IfxGtm_isEnabled(&MODULE_GTM). If the GTM is not enabled, then enable it and configure CMU clocks all inside the guard: a) call IfxGtm_enable(&MODULE_GTM); b) read the module frequency dynamically using IfxGtm_Cmu_getModuleFrequency(&MODULE_GTM); c) configure the GCLK frequency using IfxGtm_Cmu_setGclkFrequency(&MODULE_GTM, moduleFrequency); d) configure CLK0 frequency using IfxGtm_Cmu_setClkFrequency(&MODULE_GTM, IfxGtm_Cmu_Clk_0, moduleFrequency); e) enable the required clocks via IfxGtm_Cmu_enableClocks(&MODULE_GTM, appropriate clock mask). 7) Call IfxGtm_Pwm_initConfig already done earlier; then call IfxGtm_Pwm_init(&g_pwmHandle, &channelsArray[0], &config) to initialize and reserve internal driver state. 8) After initialization, apply the initial duty values stored in the persistent state by calling IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDutyImmediate(&g_pwmHandle, dutyArray) — pass the percent duty values (0..100) directly (no conversion). 9) Store persistent runtime state: keep the pwm handle, the channels array, the dutyCycles[] array and deadTimes[] array inside a module-state struct so update function can modify them later. 10) Configure any status/LED GPIO: call IfxPort_setPinModeOutput(...) to set the LED pin(s) as outputs AFTER the PWM init sequence. Notes: - All CMU/clock configuration is performed inside the enable-guard block and uses the dynamically read module frequency (no hardcoded frequency). - Duties are stored in percent (0..100) and passed directly to the runtime update API. - The initialization config and defaults are created before hardware enablement per the mandated ordering. - The function preserves original TOM/timer and pin mappings via the channel/IO assignments stored in the config (pin macros are assigned to the output config fields but are not named here).

Perform a runtime duty-cycle step update for the three configured PWM channels (U, V, W). Algorithm: 1) Read the persistent dutyCycles[] array (percent values 0..100 stored in module state). 2) For each channel i in 0..2: increment dutyCycles[i] by the configured step amount (per_update_step_percent = 10). 3) After incrementing, if dutyCycles[i] > 100.0 then set dutyCycles[i] = 0.0 (wrap-to-zero behavior). 4) After updating all three duties, call IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDutyImmediate(&g_pwmHandle, dutyRequestArray) and pass the state's dutyCycles array directly (the driver expects percent values, no scaling). 5) Do minimal computation only — no timing or polling is performed in this function (timing/periodic calling is the responsibility of Cpu0_Main). Notes: - The update function modifies only the persistent dutyCycles state and invokes the driver immediate-update API so changes take effect without delaying the caller. - The duty increment and wrap logic is identical for all three channels and treats channels as logical channels 0..2 (one per phase).
